title: rosa tomentella var. iberica
intro: Rosa tomentella var. iberica, also known as the Spanish wild rose, is a shrub native to the Iberian Peninsula and parts of Western Europe. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. Rosa tomentella var. iberica has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and even exhibiting antioxidant properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and its flowers are also used to make rose oil, which can be applied topically to soothe and calm the skin.
